user_query:
Read input/workflow_spec.yaml and input/sample_leads.csv, then design an importable n8n workflow and a runbook for our weekday lead-ingestion automation.

Deliverables:
- Write the workflow JSON to output/workflow.json (n8n importable)
- Write the runbook to output/runbook.md

Requirements:
- Trigger: every weekday (Mon–Fri) at 07:30 America/New_York.
- Source: pull the latest CSV from SFTP folder /drops/daily; the CSV has fields: email, first_name, last_name, company, created_at (ISO8601). Use the sample in input/sample_leads.csv to shape parsing and validation.
- Idempotency: make re-runs safe. Use a dedup key of email + date(created_at). Maintain a dedup state store so already-processed rows are skipped on retries. (We track state in a Google Sheet tab; refer to document ID via an environment variable, do not put secrets in JSON.)
- Processing: append only new/valid leads to a Google Sheet (doc id and tab names via env vars or credential references); send a daily summary email with counts (fetched, new, skipped, failed) and the run_id.
- Logging & audit: generate a run_id per run; log start/end, counts, and error details to an audit sheet tab.
- Error handling: add exponential backoff retries for the SFTP fetch and Sheets writes; on per-row validation or write errors, route those rows to a human-in-the-loop Review Queue tab and pause reprocessing until a reviewer marks approved.
- No silent failures: if more than 20% of rows fail validation or write, stop the line and send an alert email.
- Security: do not include any secrets in the JSON; reference credentials by name or use env-var placeholders only.
- Output format: output/workflow.json must be importable by n8n; output/runbook.md should follow a clear template and include purpose, triggers with timezone, inputs/outputs, idempotency (dedup keys and storage), error handling (retries/backoff), logging & audit (run_id), human review queue instructions, and operational checks with the threshold/stop-the-line rule.

If any destination needs clarification, assume Google Sheets for the dedup store, audit log, and review queue, with document ID supplied via an env var placeholder. Keep the workflow inactive by default.
